subject: Project Management
title: Sprint Planning
description: A fast breakdown of Sprint Planning for builders.

hook.text: Every sprint starts with planning: what will we build, and how will we build it?

buildup.text: The Product Owner presents the top backlog items. The team decides how many they can take.

discovery.text: The team breaks items into tasks, estimates effort, and commits to a sprint goal.

twist.text: The Product Owner proposes, but the team decides. Nobody outside can force more work in.

climax.text: Sprint planning aligns what the business wants with what the team can deliver — every two weeks.

punchline.text: Promise only what you can deliver. Then deliver it.

quiz.question: Who decides how much work to take into a sprint?
